docket_entries.description: REQUEST PENDING TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SZABO, TIBOR
REQUEST PENDING TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY DEFENDANT PROTACIO, MATTHEW
DECLARATION OF MATTHEW PROTACIO FILED BY DEFENDANT PROTACIO, MATTHEW

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MATTHEW PROTACIO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH OF HIS CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT APPEARS TO BE BASED SOLELY ON CC 527.6. IF THAT IS WHAT PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTS TO ALLEGE, THIS CLAIM MUST BE BROUGHT IN DEPARTMENT 505, NOT IN A CIVIL ACTION. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THIS CLAIM APPEARS TO BE BASED SOLELY ON THE EVENTS OF OCTOBER 2, 2023, AND THOSE EVENTS HAVE ALREADY BEEN PRESENTED TO JUDGE EVANGELISTA RESULTING IN NOVEMBER 30, 2023 ORDER AS EVIDENCED BY THE ASSERTIONS IN THE DECLARATION ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT AS EXHIBIT 1. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THERE ARE ONLY 3 CAUSES OF ACTION LISTED IN THE COMPLAINT, HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF REFERENCES 4 CAUSES OF ACTION IN OPPOSITION. PLAINTIFF MUST CLEARLY LABEL HIS CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT AND INCLUDE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH LABELED CAUSES OF ACTION. CONCLUSORY GENERAL STATEMENTS (E.G. "ON OR ABOUT APRIL 2024 PORTACIO IN CONCERT WITH HIS LAWYER MARC MUSANTE ARRANGED TO SET UP THE PLAINTIFF BY FILMING HIM IN HIS GALLERY; COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 23) ARE NOT SUFFICIENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLE F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Continued To Aug-06-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: In-Person Appearances

Tibor Szabo, plaintiff in pro per
Matthew Protacio, defendant in pro per

Mr. Protacio contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MATTHEW PROTACIO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each of his causes of action. The Court notes that the first cause of action for harassment appears to be based solely on CC 527.6. If that is what Plaintiff attempts to allege, this claim must be brought in department 505, not in a civil action. The Court further notes that this claim appears to be based solely on the events of October 2, 2023, and those events have already been presented to Judge Evangelista resulting in November 30, 2023 order as evidenced by the assertions in the Declaration attached to the Complaint as Exhibit 1. The Court further notes that there are only 3 causes of action listed in the Complaint, however, Plaintiff references 4 causes of action in opposition. Plaintiff must clearly label his causes of action in the amended complaint and include facts in support of each element of each labeled causes of action. Conclusory general statements (e.g. "On or about April 2024 Portacio in concert with his lawyer Marc Musante arranged to set up the plaintiff by filming him in his gallery; Complaint, paragraph 23) are not sufficient.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charle F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Continued To Feb-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT MATTHEW PROTACIO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on February 10, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all landlord/tenant disputes per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than January 31, 2025.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HK)
